methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $171M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Performance Drone Works, founded by Ryan Gury, is a Series B stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The company has raised $171M to date. The valuation exercise draws on a comp universe of 8 private peers in drones and autonomous systems, including Elroy Air, Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, and Archer Aviation. No public comparables exist in this narrow segment.

A peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables meeting standard filtering thresholds—only 1 same-stage peer was identified across the 5 total sector peers, falling short of the minimum 3 datapoints required for statistical reliability. Consequently, valuation relies on a stage-progression method, which applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B to later-stage transitions. Using the $171M total raised as a valuation proxy, this method implies a valuation of $325M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $276M (low) to $374M (high), with a median estimate of $325M. This range reflects sensitivity around the 1.9x step-up assumption. Key uncertainties include the limited comparability set—applying all-sector medians risks noise from heterogeneous business models—and the reliance on raised capital as a valuation anchor, which may not reflect current market conditions for autonomous systems or stage-specific risk factors specific to Performance Drone Works.
